summary: Comprehensive analysis of non-AI improvements across 10 categories. Found 42 actionable improvements with varying effort/priority.

rule_based_conversion.what: Enhanced dialogue attribution with expanded speech verb lexicon
rule_based_conversion.why: Current regex patterns in rules_engine.py only detect ~20 speech verbs. Missing common verbs like 'murmured', 'hissed', 'stammered', 'interjected', etc. leads to misattributed dialogue.
rule_based_conversion.how: Expand _DIALOGUE_ATTRIB_AFTER_RE and _DIALOGUE_ATTRIB_BEFORE_RE patterns with 50+ speech verbs. Add possessive forms ('John's voice said'). Implement fallback to nearest capitalized word when no verb match.

rule_based_conversion.what: Scene break detection using lexical chains
rule_based_conversion.why: Current scoring system (_is_scene_break) uses simple keyword matching. Missing subtle scene transitions indicated by entity continuity breaks, temporal progression, or location changes.
rule_based_conversion.how: Implement lexical chain analysis: track named entities across paragraphs, detect when entity sets change significantly. Add sentiment shift detection as scene boundary signal. Use TF-IDF to identify topic shifts.

rule_based_conversion.what: Character name extraction using frequency analysis
rule_based_conversion.why: Current extraction relies on ALL CAPS patterns and dialogue tags. Misses characters introduced in action lines or with inconsistent capitalization.
rule_based_conversion.how: Implement frequency-based extraction: scan for capitalized words appearing 3+ times. Use co-occurrence analysis with dialogue tags. Add heuristics for 'Mr./Mrs./Dr.' prefixes. Consider using spaCy NER when available.

rule_based_conversion.why: Current _INT_KEYWORDS and _EXT_KEYWORDS contain ~50 locations each. Missing many common locations (hospital rooms, vehicle types, outdoor settings).
rule_based_conversion.how: Expand keyword lists to 200+ locations each. Add hierarchical location detection (e.g., 'hospital' -> 'hospital room', 'hospital bed'). Use WordNet or similar to find location synonyms. Add context-aware detection (e.g., 'drove' implies vehicle).

rule_based_conversion.what: Support for nested dialogue and interruptions
rule_based_conversion.why: Current DialogueExtractor doesn't handle nested quotes ('He said,
rule_based_conversion.how: Implement stack-based quote matching for nesting. Add detection of em-dash interruptions. Handle interrupted dialogue with proper Fountain continuation markers. Add support for dialogue-within-dialogue formatting.

rule_based_conversion.what: Better handling of Fountain elements in conversion
rule_based_conversion.why: Current rules_engine only generates scene headings, action, character, dialogue. Missing centered text, sections, synopses, lyrics, page breaks.
rule_based_conversion.how: Add detection patterns for centered text (>text<), sections (#heading), synopses (=text), lyrics (~text). Implement element-specific formatting rules. Add page break detection at chapter boundaries.

performance.what: Parallel chapter conversion for multi-core utilization
performance.why: Current convert_novel processes chapters sequentially. For LLM mode, each chapter waits for API response. Parallel processing could reduce total time by 50-70%.
performance.how: Implement concurrent.futures.ProcessPoolExecutor for rules mode. For AI mode, use asyncio with rate limiting. Add progress tracking for parallel operations. Consider chapter batching for API efficiency.

performance.why: Current loader reads entire file into memory. For large novels (1MB+), this causes high memory usage and slow loading.
performance.how: Implement streaming loaders for PDF, EPUB, DOCX. Use generators for chapter splitting. Add chunked processing for very large files. Consider memory mapping for text files.

performance.why: Current ConversionCache uses content-hash but stores in memory. For repeated conversions of same text, could use LRU cache with size limits.
performance.how: Implement functools.lru_cache for pure functions. Add disk-based LRU cache with size limits. Cache parsed Fountain output for re-rendering. Add cache statistics for monitoring.

performance.why: spaCy and other NLP models loaded on import. For rules-only mode, this wastes memory and startup time.
performance.how: Implement lazy imports for optional NLP dependencies. Use importlib for conditional loading. Add model availability checks. Provide fallbacks when models unavailable.

ux_ui.what: Accessibility improvements with ARIA labels and keyboard navigation
ux_ui.why: Current frontend lacks comprehensive ARIA labels. Keyboard navigation incomplete for complex components like file upload, conversion panel.
ux_ui.how: Add aria-label, aria-describedby, aria-live regions. Implement focus management for step transitions. Add keyboard shortcuts for common actions. Ensure proper heading hierarchy. Add skip navigation links.

ux_ui.why: Current CSS has mobile breakpoints but some components don't adapt well. File upload, conversion panel, export panel need mobile optimization.
ux_ui.how: Implement mobile-first CSS. Add touch-friendly interactions. Optimize form layouts for small screens. Add swipe gestures for navigation. Improve text readability on mobile.

ux_ui.why: Current conversion shows generic 'Converting...' message. No indication of progress, current chapter, or estimated time remaining.
ux_ui.how: Implement WebSocket or SSE for progress updates. Add progress bar with percentage. Show current chapter being processed. Add estimated time remaining. Implement cancel functionality.

ux_ui.why: Current file upload accepts single file. For multi-chapter novels, users might want to reorder chapters before conversion.
ux_ui.how: Add multi-file upload with drag-and-drop reordering. Implement chapter preview for each file. Add merge functionality. Store order in local storage.

developer_experience.why: Current codebase has type hints but not strict mypy checking. Some functions lack return types, optional handling inconsistent.
developer_experience.how: Enable mypy strict mode in pyproject.toml. Add missing type annotations. Fix Any types. Add TypeVar for generic functions. Implement py.typed marker. Add pre-commit mypy hook.

developer_experience.why: No pre-commit hooks for code formatting, linting, type checking. Code style inconsistent across files.
developer_experience.how: Add .pre-commit-config.yaml with black, isort, flake8, mypy. Add linting rules for Python and TypeScript. Implement auto-format on commit. Add commit message linting.

developer_experience.why: No automated testing, linting, or deployment. Manual processes error-prone.
developer_experience.how: Create .github/workflows/ci.yml with test, lint, typecheck steps. Add matrix testing for Python 3.10-3.12. Implement coverage reporting. Add dependency caching. Add release automation.

developer_experience.why: README is comprehensive but no API documentation. Internal functions undocumented.
developer_experience.how: Add Sphinx documentation with autodoc. Generate API docs from docstrings. Add tutorial documentation. Implement docs CI/CD. Add interactive examples.

export_quality.why: Current PDF export uses basic fpdf2 with hardcoded values. Missing proper screenplay typography: Courier font metrics, proper line spacing, margin consistency.
export_quality.how: Implement proper Courier 12pt metrics (10 CPI, 6 LPI). Add proper line height calculations. Fix margin inconsistencies. Add proper page numbering. Implement continued markers correctly.

export_quality.why: Current validator has 20 error codes but missing some Fountain 1.1 features. Some edge cases not handled.
export_quality.how: Add validation for forced scene headings (.TEXT), forced action (!TEXT), forced character (@TEXT). Add boneyard and note validation. Implement title page validation. Add more comprehensive transition validation.

export_quality.what: Support for more Fountain elements in PDF
export_quality.why: Current PDF export only handles basic elements. Missing centered text, sections, synopses, lyrics, page breaks.
export_quality.how: Add rendering for centered text (>text<). Add section headings (#). Add synopsis (=). Add lyrics (~). Add page breaks (===). Implement proper formatting for each.

export_quality.why: Current page_estimator.py uses simple line counting. Not accounting for element-specific formatting differences.
export_quality.how: Implement element-aware page counting: action lines (58 chars/line), dialogue (18 chars/line). Add proper line wrapping calculations. Consider element spacing. Add confidence intervals.

ingestion.what: Support for RTF and ODT formats
ingestion.why: Current loader only supports txt, md, pdf, epub, docx. Missing common formats like RTF (Rich Text Format) and ODT (OpenDocument Text).
ingestion.how: Add RTF parser using rtfparse or similar. Add ODT parser using odfpy. Implement format detection. Add proper text extraction with formatting preservation.

ingestion.why: Current PDF extraction uses PyMuPDF but doesn't handle images, tables, or complex layouts. Extracted text may be garbled.
ingestion.how: Add OCR support for scanned PDFs using Tesseract. Implement layout analysis to preserve reading order. Add table extraction. Handle multi-column layouts. Add image text extraction.

ingestion.why: Current _detect_encoding in cli.py uses simple heuristic. May misdetect encodings for non-UTF-8 files.
ingestion.how: Implement chardet or charset-normalizer for better detection. Add BOM detection. Handle multiple encoding attempts. Add user override option.

ingestion.why: Current loader reads entire file into memory. For very large files (10MB+), this causes memory issues.
ingestion.how: Implement streaming loaders using generators. Add chunked processing. Add progress indicators for large files. Implement memory-efficient parsing.

validation.why: Current validator has 20 error codes but missing some Fountain 1.1 features. Not checking all edge cases.
validation.how: Add validation for: scene heading time-of-day variations (MOVING, FLASHBACK), character extensions (V.O., O.S., CONT'D), transition variations (CUT TO BLACK, DISSOLVE TO), forced elements, centered text, sections, synopses, lyrics, page breaks.

validation.why: Current auto_fix only handles basic fixes. Many common errors could be automatically corrected.
validation.how: Add auto-fix for: missing INT./EXT. prefix, missing time-of-day, uncapitalized character names, improper transitions, missing dialogue indentation. Add confidence scoring for fixes.

validation.why: Current validator doesn't check title page formatting. Invalid title pages can cause parsing issues.
validation.how: Add validation for: required fields (Title, Author), proper formatting, date format, contact information. Add warning for missing fields.

validation.why: Current validator focuses on format errors but not content mistakes. Common screenwriting errors could be flagged.
validation.how: Add checks for: camera directions in action lines, 'WE SEE'/'WE HEAR' phrases, excessive parentheticals, long action blocks, scene headings without time-of-day. Add educational warnings.

testing.why: Current test coverage unknown. Some modules may have low coverage. Edge cases not tested.
testing.how: Run coverage analysis. Add tests for uncovered code paths. Add edge case tests. Add integration tests. Add performance tests. Set coverage thresholds in CI.

testing.what: Add integration tests for end-to-end conversion
testing.why: Current tests are unit tests. No tests for complete conversion pipeline from input to output.
testing.how: Add end-to-end tests: load document -> convert -> validate -> export. Test with various input formats. Test with different conversion modes. Add regression tests.

testing.why: No performance tests. Unknown performance characteristics for large documents.
testing.how: Add benchmark tests using pytest-benchmark. Test conversion speed for various document sizes. Test memory usage. Add performance regression detection.

testing.what: Add edge case tests for unusual inputs
testing.why: Current tests use standard inputs. Unusual inputs (empty files, malformed text, special characters) not tested.
testing.how: Add tests for: empty files, very large files, special characters, Unicode, malformed Fountain, corrupted files. Add property-based testing.

packaging.why: No Docker support. Difficult to deploy consistently across environments.
packaging.how: Create Dockerfile with Python base image. Add docker-compose.yml for development. Include frontend build. Add health checks. Add volume mounts for data.

packaging.what: PyPI packaging with proper entry points
packaging.why: Current packaging via hatchling but not published to PyPI. Missing proper distribution setup.
packaging.how: Add proper package metadata. Create sdist and wheel distributions. Add twine for upload. Implement version bumping. Add changelog generation.

packaging.why: Frontend built separately but not packaged for distribution. No npm module for reuse.
packaging.how: Add package.json with proper metadata. Build as npm module. Add TypeScript declarations. Implement tree shaking. Add peer dependencies.

packaging.why: Current installation requires Python. No standalone executables for end users.
packaging.how: Create PyInstaller specs for Windows, macOS, Linux. Add code signing for macOS. Implement auto-update mechanism. Add installer scripts.

P1: Enhanced dialogue attribution with expanded speech verb lexicon
Scene break detection using lexical chains
Character name extraction using frequency analysis
Better handling of Fountain elements in conversion
Parallel chapter conversion for multi-core utilization
Accessibility improvements with ARIA labels and keyboard navigation
Add integration tests for end-to-end conversion
Add edge case tests for unusual inputs
PyPI packaging with proper entry points
P2: Support for nested dialogue and interruptions
Support for more Fountain elements in PDF
Support for RTF and ODT formats

Low: Enhanced dialogue attribution with expanded speech verb lexicon
PyPI packaging with proper entry points
Medium: Scene break detection using lexical chains
Character name extraction using frequency analysis
Better handling of Fountain elements in conversion
Parallel chapter conversion for multi-core utilization

Support for RTF and ODT formats
Add integration tests for end-to-end conversion
Add edge case tests for unusual inputs
High: Support for nested dialogue and interruptions

evidence.note: Dialogue attribution regex only detects ~20 speech verbs, missing common literary verbs
Scene break detection uses simple scoring without lexical chain analysis
Character name extraction relies on ALL CAPS patterns, missing mixed-case names
Missing support for centered text, sections, synopses, lyrics, page breaks
Sequential chapter conversion without parallel processing
No streaming loading for large files, entire file read into memory
Hardcoded PDF margins and spacing without proper Courier metrics
Missing validation for forced elements, title page, content mistakes
Mobile responsive design exists but some components don't adapt well
No integration tests, performance tests, or edge case tests

blockers: Need to assess current test coverage before prioritizing testing improvements
Some improvements depend on optional dependencies (spaCy for NLP, chardet for encoding)
Frontend improvements require design decisions before implementation
next_questions: What is the current test coverage percentage?
Are there any specific performance bottlenecks identified in production?
What are the most common user complaints about the conversion quality?
